content.claim: Early academic selection in Hungary begins at Grade 5 (age 10-11) for 8-year tracks and Grade 7 (age 12-13) for 6-year tracks, creating partial tracking earlier than OECD average practice
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Describes structural availability, not actual enrollment proportions or student flow into each track
Does not establish that early timing itself causes segregation independent of selectivity criterion
Does not differentiate uptake rates by socioeconomic background
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
